known_bottlenecks.load: 8× load_offset per group → 4 cycles (load×2 slot limit)
known_bottlenecks.key_insight: load 引擎在 hash 阶段 9c 完全空闲 → cross-group pipeline 机会

attempts.reasoning: 当前实现 18c/group 的核心瓶颈是：load 阶段（5c）和 hash 阶段（9c）串行执行，且两者使用完全不同的引擎（load engine vs valu engine）。VLIW 架构允许不同引擎在同一 cycle 并行执行，因此将相邻两组 (A,B) 交错：当 A 在 hash（valu-only, 9c）时，B 同时 load（load engine, 4c）。理论上每对 group 从 36c 降至 ~24c（12c/group），总 cycles ≈ 16轮×16对×24 ≈ 6,144。实现关键：为每个 group 独立分配 addr_tmp/nv_tmp，避免 WAW 冲突；利用 build() 的自动打包逻辑，只需按正确拓扑顺序交错提交 slots。预期从 9,936 降至 ~6,200，改善约 37%。

attempts.progress: 完成 per-group scratch 分配和 helper 函数，正在交错循环体

bottleneck_analysis.primary_bottleneck: valu chain: hash computation remains serial within each group; 12 valu slots with 3 multiply_add dependency chains
bottleneck_analysis.load_utilization: Improved — B group loads overlap A hash phase (2 load slots × ~4 cycles during A hash); still only 2 load slots per cycle limit
bottleneck_analysis.valu_utilization: Partially improved via 2-way cross-pipeline; hash s1/s3/s5 pairs (2 parallel slots) fill 2 of 6 valu slots, multiply_add uses 1 slot — max ~2-3/6 utilized during hash phase
bottleneck_analysis.theoretical_min_cycles: hash valu chain: 12 slots with dependencies → min ~8 cycles if all non-dep pairs packed; 8 loads/group ÷ 2 load-slots = 4 cycles; 16 rounds × 32 groups ÷ 2 (paired) × ~12 cycles/pair ≈ 3072 cycles lower bound
bottleneck_analysis.headroom: 6096 → ~3072 theoretical; 2x headroom still available via 4-way pipeline (A-load overlaps C-hash while B-idx overlaps D-addr) or tighter hash interleaving

attempts.next_round_recommendation: 4-way cross-pipeline (ABCD groups): cycle A.hash + B.load + C.idx + D.addr simultaneously. Also try packing multiply_add with parallel independent valu ops across groups in same bundle to push valu utilization above 3/6.

parallel_plans: 002a: 3-group incremental — process groups in triples (10 triples + 1 pair), add pair_2 scratch for group C temps, C.load overlaps A.hash window, expected ~5,200 cycles (10c/group)

002b: 4-group aggressive — process groups in quads (8 quads, 32/4 exact), add pair_2 and pair_3 scratch, A.hash + B.load + C.idx + D.addr all overlap simultaneously, target detailed 20-step interleave schedule, expected ~3,200 cycles (6.5c/group)
attempts.reasoning: Round 1 achieved 6,096 cycles (11.9c/group) via 2-way cross-pipeline where B.load overlaps A.hash. The Evaluator confirmed valu utilization is only 2-3/6 slots during hash, and load engine is idle during A.idx phase (5 cycles). The key insight for Route 2a: during the 9-cycle A.hash window, the load engine is completely idle — injecting C.load (8 slots × 2/cycle = 4 cycles) and C.addr (1 valu slot) costs nothing extra. This turns a 2×12=24c pair into a 3×~10=30c triple. Route 2b goes further: with 4 groups, every engine cycle has work — A.hash (valu), B.load (load), C.idx (valu), D.addr (valu) can all pack into the same VLIW bundle. The 6-valu-slot budget is 1-2+1-2+1=3-5 slots, staying under the limit. 4-way divides 32 groups cleanly (8 quads) with no remainder handling. Theoretical: ~3,072 cycles (Evaluator confirmed). Route 2a is low-risk (incremental change to loop body only), Route 2b is high-reward (near-theoretical minimum) with higher implementation complexity. Both routes add only 2-4 new scratch pair allocations and change only the main loop body structure — the 5 helper functions remain unchanged.

attempts.note: cycles confirmed by direct test run in worktree

bottleneck_analysis.primary_bottleneck: tree traversal depth / serial dependency chain per sample
bottleneck_analysis.headroom: Still 3x above opus45 best; further SIMD across samples or cross-group pipeline needed

bottleneck_analysis.primary_bottleneck: flow slot (1 branch/cycle) limiting loop iterations; valu/alu underutilized in tree traversal
bottleneck_analysis.headroom: 002b is 35x over baseline but still 3x above opus45 target; aggressive loop unroll + deeper SIMD pipelining should push below 2000

attempts.reasoning: 当前 4-group pipeline 在 32.6 cycles/quad 下运行，详细 bundle 分析显示实际已接近理论值（~32 bundles/quad vs 31 理论最小）。主要 overhead 来自两处：(1) 每个 quad 的 tail：D.idx 串行链（5 steps）产生 bundles 27-31，每个只有 1-2 valu slots，浪费大量 capacity；(2) 每个 quad 的 startup：A.addr + A.load 开始之前 B-D 未就绪。8-group octet pipeline 通过 E-H 组填充这些空洞来解决：E.addr/E.load 在 A-D hash window（steps 4-18）期间进行，H.idx tail 与 D.idx tail 合并到同一个 diagonal 中。结构上：32 groups / 8 = 4 octets，16 rounds × 4 = 64 octets。额外 scratch：4 pairs × 4 × 8 = 128 words，总计 1473 < 1536。预期 cycles：~2200-2500（当前 4176 的 40-47% 降幅）。idx_update 链深度为 5（lsb→offset→new_idx→cmp→idx 全为真 RAW），无法通过指令重排减少；cross-round 流水线同样被 idx→addr 的 RAW 依赖阻断。因此 8-group octet pipeline 是本轮唯一有效的大幅优化方向。

bottleneck_analysis.valu_in_load_cycles_utilization: 41.1% (only 2.47/6 valu slots used during load cycles)
bottleneck_analysis.critical_insight: 1091 cycles (34.7%) have idle load engine. During 2048 load cycles, valu engine is only 41% utilized. The 35-step octet schedule produces 49 actual cycles/octet (not 35) because build() greedy packer cannot perfectly pack the manually-crafted schedule. There are 5056 unused valu slots during load cycles - far more than the 4672 valu slots done in no-load cycles. The fundamental waste: load and valu operations interleave poorly because the dependency chain (addr->load->xor->hash->idx) creates sequential phases where only one engine is busy.
bottleneck_analysis.path_to_target: 1487 < 2048 load minimum. To break this barrier: (1) Eliminate loads for early rounds using vselect - rounds 0,11 need 0 loads (all idx=0, broadcast), rounds 1,12 need 2 loads+vselect, reducing ~25% of loads. BUT vselect adds valu ops shifting bottleneck to valu (already at 1621 min). (2) Wider pipeline: 16-group pipeline would use scratch=1471 (fits!) with 8 pair temps; critical path analysis shows 35 cycles per 16-group batch, same as 8-group, BUT better load/valu interleaving could achieve near 64 cycles per 16-group (full load utilization). (3) Combined: better VLIW packing (pack setup ops) + 16-group pipeline + early-round special case. Key insight: the periodic idx reset at rounds 0 and 11 (all elements reset to idx=0 after reaching leaves) is the key structural property to exploit.
bottleneck_analysis.structural_discovery: idx values are PERIODIC: all elements start at idx=0 (round 0), traverse the tree for 10 rounds, then ALL reset to idx=0 at round 11 (because all leaf nodes produce 2*idx+(1or2) >= n_nodes=2047). This creates an 11-round cycle that repeats. Rounds 0 and 11 have ALL elements with idx=0 (only 1 tree node needed). Rounds 1,12 have at most 2 unique idx values. Rounds 2,13 at most 4. Rounds 3,14 at most 8. Only rounds 4-10 require full 8-per-group loads.

attempts.round4_recommendation: THREE TRACKS IN PRIORITY ORDER: (1) SETUP PACKING [easy, ~100 cycle savings]: Replace all add() calls in setup with build()-based batching. Pack const loads 2/cycle (currently 1 each), pack vbroadcasts 6/cycle (currently 1 each). Reduces setup from 141 to ~50 cycles. (2) WIDER PIPELINE [medium, ~900 cycle savings]: Implement 16-group or 32-group pipeline instead of 8-group. Key advantage: better load/valu interleaving with more groups in flight. With 8 pair temps (128 extra scratch words, fits in 193 free), can handle 16 groups safely. Design explicit schedule for 16-group batch targeting 64-70 cycles per batch (vs current 49 cycles per 8-group = 98 for same 16 groups). (3) EARLY ROUND SPECIAL CASING [hard, potentially reach target]: For rounds 0 and 11 (idx=0 for all), use 1 scalar load + broadcast instead of 8 load_offset per group. For rounds 1,12 (idx in {1,2}), use 2 scalar loads + 1 vselect per group. For rounds 2,13 (idx in {3..6}), use 4 loads + vselect tree. This replaces 1536 load ops with ~100 loads + extra valu. BUT: valu becomes bottleneck (estimated 1800+ min cycles). The path to <1487 REQUIRES BOTH: a) reducing early-round loads (rounds 0-3,11-14) from 2048 to ~400 load ops AND b) reducing hash valu ops (cannot use multiply_add for stages 1,3,5 which cost 3 ops each). Possible hash optimization: eliminate zero_vec by using alu for idx bounds check instead of valu. MOST PROMISING: Attack setup overhead first (low risk), then try 16-group pipeline, measure result. If that achieves ~2000 cycles, then add early-round special casing.

attempts.reasoning: After careful verification: ALL 256 elements start with idx=0 (confirmed in frozen_problem.py Input.generate()). Tree height=10, n_nodes=2047. The period is exactly 11 rounds: after round 10, all leaf indices produce 2*idx+(1or2)>=2047, so ALL reset to 0. With rounds=16: rounds 0,11 have idx=0; rounds 1,12 have idx∈{1,2}; rounds 2,13 have idx∈{3,4,5,6}; rounds 3,14 have idx∈{7..14}; rounds 4-10,15 are normal. THREE parallel optimizations: (A) Setup packing: 12 vbroadcasts packed 6/cycle saves ~90 cycles from 141-cycle setup. (B) 16-group pipeline: extends 8-group octet to 16-group, better load/valu interleaving, targets ~67 cycles per 16-group batch vs current 98 cycles (2×49) for same 16 groups → estimates ~2285 total. (C) Early-round special-casing for rounds 0,11 (1 load + shared vbroadcast + 32 xor = ~104 cycles vs 196), rounds 1,12 (2 loads + 32 vselect-on-idx + hash + idx = ~140 cycles), rounds 2,13 (4 loads + 64 vselect + hash + idx = ~160 cycles). Combined estimate: ~1815 cycles. If still above 1487, extend to 32-group pipeline. Key correctness constraint: for type_1 vselect (round 1,12), the vselect condition is on CURRENT idx (not hashed val) — idx∈{1,2}, so lsb of idx=1 selects node[1], lsb of idx=0 selects node[2]. Two parallel tracks: (004a) 16-group pipeline + setup packing; (004b) early-round special-cases on existing 8-group + setup packing. Measure both, then combine winner.
parallel_plans: 004b: early-round special-cases (rounds 0,1,2,11,12,13) on 8-group + setup packing — expected ~2600 cycles

attempts.note: 16-group hextet pipeline + setup packing. Frozen problem confirms 3132 cycles. 94 actual cycles/hextet vs 98 for two sequential octets (4 cycle saving per hextet). Tail issue: I-P hash+idx runs 27 cycles pure valu after all loads done.

attempts.reject_reason: correctness failure: vsel4_nv_hi and vsel4_b_in_lo are SHARED scratch across all 8 groups in octet, causing WAW corruption — d_vsx4[2] overwrites vsel4_nv_hi before d_vsx4[4] reads it when e_vsx4[2] interleaves. Round mod 2 (round_mod==2) special-casing broken.

bottleneck_analysis.notes: 16-group hextet pipeline: 32 groups in 2 hextets of 16. 3132/32=97.9c/hextet. Normal rounds use 8-octet pipeline (slower). Early rounds (mod 0,1,2) are special-cased in 004b design but broken.

attempts.round5_recommendation: Fix 004b early-round special-casing bug: use per-group dedicated scratch for vsel4_nv_hi/vsel4_b_in_lo instead of shared. Also apply 16-group hextet pipeline to NORMAL rounds in 004b (currently falls back to 8-octet). Combined target: ~2500-2800 cycles.

attempts.cancel_reason: premature launch: 004b background agent not yet completed

attempts.key_insight: Special round pipeline (8-group) is slower than hextet; need to combine 16-group pipeline with special xor for real benefit

key_optimizations: round_mod==0 (rounds 0,11): broadcast single forest_values[0], skip 32x8=256 load_offsets
round_mod==1 (rounds 1,12): 2-node vselect using per-group addr_tmp_g as lsb_cond scratch
bugs_fixed_vs_004b: noload hextet uses correct normal-hextet diagonal (pair-safe ordering)
vselect hextet uses per-group addr_tmp_g (not shared lsb_tmp_pair)
setup slots emitted as (engine,slot) tuples into body (correct format)

bottleneck_analysis.mod1_bottleneck: 16 flow vselects serialized = 16c floor per hextet

attempts.round6_recommendation: Option C+A: Replace flow vselect with valu multiply_add trick for mod1 rounds (eliminate 16c flow serialization bottleneck per hextet, save ~64c for 2 mod1 rounds). Then extend to mod2 special-casing using 2-level arithmetic vselect: bit0=idx&1 for lo/hi, bit1=(idx-3)>>1 for final select, 8 valu ops/group vs 8 load_offset/group but at 6x throughput. Estimated total savings ~170c -> target ~2796 cycles.

attempts.progress: Optimization 2 (mod2 4-node arith) done: 2966→2908 cycles. Starting Opt 1 (mod1 valu vselect).

optimizations_implemented: mod1 valu arithmetic vselect: replaced flow.vselect with multiply_add arithmetic (lsb * diff + node2)
mod2 4-node arithmetic select: replaced 8x load_offset with 8-step valu arithmetic using bit decomposition
attempts.notes: mod2 arith4 required careful quartet-based emission ordering to prevent pair-reuse WAW hazard

attempts.note: accepted despite <5% improvement; positive direction confirmed

bottleneck_analysis.path_to_1487: Normal rounds at 1840 (63%) dominate; need reduction from 184→42 per round to hit target with current special round costs – nearly impossible by load physics. Real path: combine special-casing more rounds (mod3,mod4) + hextet tail optimization + possibly setup reduction.
bottleneck_analysis.hextet_tail_waste: ~5-9 cycles at tail (P group finishing while others done)
bottleneck_analysis.forest_precache_blocked: Scratch space insufficient: need 1023 forest vals + 791 min scratch = 1814 > 1536 limit

attempts.round7_recommendation: Route B+A2: (1) Optimize normal hextet tail/head scheduling to reduce 92->~75 cycles/hextet (save ~340 total), estimated 2570 cycles. (2) Special-case mod3 (8 nodes, bit decomposition with 3 valu ops/group), save ~90 more, reach ~2480. Combined: A2+B is most viable single-round improvement. Route B alone (hextet optimization) is most impactful: close the 28-cycle/hextet overhead gap.

analysis.conclusion: arith8 is 11c/hextet SLOWER than normal; deep dependency chain negates load elimination

optimizations_implemented: emit_hextet_arith8 function defined (but reverted to not use it)
scratch allocation for mod3 nodes kept but unused in hot path
attempts.notes: round_mod==3 falls through to normal hextet (else branch). No cycle reduction.

attempts.reject_reason: no improvement: arith8 dep-chain 14 bundles/quartet vs 7 for arith4, actually 11c/hextet slower

optimizations_implemented: intra-round hextet tail/head overlap: hextet0 tail prefetches hextet1 A+B+C+D addr+loads
inter-round tail/head overlap: hextet1 tail prefetches next normal round hextet0 A+B+C+D addr+loads
arith4 (mod2) tail overlap: prefetches next normal round hextet0 A+B+C+D addr+loads
D.loads overlap with valu ops in hextet body (steps 3-6)
prev_prefetched flag: all 10 normal rounds save ~13 cycles per hextet by skipping the head

bottleneck_analysis.path_to_1487: Would need 1487 from 2668 = reduce by 1181 cycles. Normal rounds: 10x32=320 possible. Special rounds: mod0+11=2x~50=100. Remaining gap 1181-420=761 not achievable with current architecture. Need fundamentally different approach - possibly larger hextet groups (32-group) or reducing hash chain depth.

attempts.round8_recommendation: Focus on reducing normal hextet from 80→64 cycles. The load bottleneck is 64 cycles for 128 load_offset ops. Current 80c vs 64c theoretical = 16 cycles overhead per hextet. Root cause: VALU bottleneck in hash diagonal leaves load engine idle at step boundaries. Counter-strategy: compress hash chain further OR interleave more groups. Scratch has 105 words remaining - enough for 13 more VLEN=8 scratch vectors. Most viable: 32-group hextet (3 hextets per round at 64c each = 192c, but saves vs 2x80=160 for 2 hextets... actually worse). Alternative: reduce addr overhead - addr is 1 valu per group, 16 per hextet = 3 cycles; can we precompute? Another option: fuse the two hextets into one 32-group hextet to benefit from more overlap. Expected 007b result was 2,668 (same), suggesting this inter-hextet overlap is the current state-of-art without deeper restructuring.

attempts.notes: Layer 1 complete: addr-to-front optimization + intra_hextet_s. Normal rounds now 157c. Further improvement needed.

Added intra_tail_next_s: C[6:8]+D loads in hextet0 tail
d_preloaded=True in hextet1 body: skips D loads, removes step 6 overflow

L1: Applied to emit_hextet_noload tail (saves ~2c/round for mod0)
L1: Applied to emit_hextet_arith4 tail (saves ~2c/round for mod2->normal)
L1: Applied to emit_hextet_tail_steps50_63 (saves ~2c/round for all normal rounds)

attempts.analysis: Gap to 2164c (next threshold) = 474c. Normal rounds still at 157c (load bound 128c requires eliminating 29 idle load cycles per round). Main bottleneck: diagonal structure causes 4-load overflows at group boundaries, wastes ~13 cycles per hextet. Full restructuring needed for >300c improvement.

fundamental_analysis.note: 1689c valu floor (10134 main-loop valu ops / 6) + 120c setup = 1809c. Load (1287c) is NOT the bottleneck - valu is.

waste_breakdown.special_rounds_nearly_zero_load: rounds 0-2 use 430c with <6 total load ops → load engine idle 424c
waste_breakdown.valu_dep_chain_overhead: 10134 valu ops need 1689c minimum but take 2225c actual (1.32x overhead)
waste_breakdown.valu_idle_during_loads: 297 cycles post-setup have zero valu ops (11.8%), 225 of those are load-active
waste_breakdown.per_hextet_gap: noload hextets: 62c actual vs 48c theoretical (1.31x overhead from dep chains)

fundamental_analysis.why_1487_is_currently_unreachable: Target 1487c requires main loop in 1367c. That's 1367x6=8202 valu slots. But main loop has 10134 valu ops - mathematically impossible to fit 10134 ops in 8202 slots. Each group-iteration needs irreducible minimum of 19 valu ops (addr+xor+hash12+idx5). Reducing below 19/group is not possible without changing the algorithm.
fundamental_analysis.path_to_improvement: The 430c special-round penalty (rounds 0-2: pure valu, zero loads) must be overlapped with normal-round loads via cross-round pipelining. With full diagonal unroll, special round valu runs during normal round load time. Theoretical: 1805c. Practical: 1850-2100c achievable.
fundamental_analysis.new_direction: Full cross-round diagonal pipeline: emit all 32x16=512 group-iterations in topological dependency order (group g, round k can start as soon as group g, round k-1 completes its idx_update). This breaks the round-sequential structure that keeps load engine idle for 430c during special rounds.

attempts.round9_recommendation: Implement full 32x16 diagonal unroll. Sort all (group, round) pairs by topological order. For each pair, emit: if normal round (mod>=3): addr + load + xor + hash + idx; if special round: xor_special + hash + idx. The build() VLIW packer will automatically interleave load ops from normal-round groups with valu ops from special-round groups. Expected: ~1900-2100c (vs 2638c, 20-28% improvement). This requires refactoring the main loop from 'for round in rounds: emit all 32 groups' to 'emit all 512 iterations in diagonal order'.

attempts.reasoning:
Planner Brain Round 9 Analysis (rerun):

VALU OP COUNT: 10,134 total (confirmed match with evaluator).
  mod0 x2 rounds: 1,154 valu (577/round = 32 groups * 18 ops/group)
  mod1 x2 rounds: 1,286 valu (643/round = 32 groups * 20 ops)
  mod2 x2 rounds: 1,614 valu (807/round = 32 groups * 25 ops)
  normal x10 rounds: 6,080 valu (608/round = 32 groups * 19 ops)

ALTERNATIVES CONSIDERED AND REJECTED:
1. Switch mod1/mod2 to load-based: saves 448 valu ops but adds 1024 load ops.
   New load min = 1792c > valu min 1689c. Combined min = 1912c > current 1809c. WORSE.
2. Eliminate addr_compute: load_offset reads scratch[base+lane] as RAW address.
   Cannot encode forest_values_ptr + idx[g][lane] as separate base+offset. IMPOSSIBLE.

CORRECT APPROACH: Keep ALL current ops unchanged. Change ONLY emission order.

FUNDAMENTAL INSIGHT: Groups are completely independent. Within a group, rounds
are sequential (round k depends on round k-1 idx). In current round-major order:
ALL groups finish round k before ANY group starts round k+1. This causes:
- Special rounds 0,1,2 = pure valu, load engine idle for ~430c
- Normal rounds have loads but special round valu cannot run simultaneously

GROUP-MAJOR DIAGONAL: for g in 0..31: for r in 0..15: emit per_group_round_slots(g, r)
- Group 0 finishes rounds 0,1,2 (pure valu, ~11c) then starts round 3 (loads)
- Groups 1-31 still doing rounds 0-2 (pure valu) while group 0 does round 3 loads
- The packer packs group 0 loads + groups 1-31 valu in same VLIW bundles
- Special-round valu (4054 ops) fills normal-round load-idle slots

1487c TARGET: MATHEMATICALLY IMPOSSIBLE. True floor = 1809c (10134/6 + 120 setup).
Best realistic target: 1850-2200c. True stretch: approaching 1809c.

IMPLEMENTATION: Replace all hextet emission with group-major per_group_round_slots().
All special-round setup must move to init (before pause). Keep 4-pair scheme.
Scratch budget: ~1432/1536 used, no expansion needed.

attempts.finding: group-major emission order causes the greedy left-to-right packer to serialize group 0 all 16 rounds before starting group 1. No cross-group interleaving occurs. 9864 cycles vs 2638 baseline. Plan hypothesis was flawed: for packer interleaving to work, slots from group 1 must appear immediately after group 0 round N, but group-major places them only after all 32 rounds of group 0.

attempts.root_cause: group-major loop: build() greedy packer cannot overlap group 0 round 3 loads with group 1-31 round 0 valu because group 0 all 16 rounds are emitted before group 1 starts. Slots are not interleaved in time.

attempts.reject_reason: massive regression: 9864 vs 2638. Group-major emission fails because build() is a greedy left-to-right packer - it cannot interleave group 0 round 3 loads with group 1-31 round 0 valu. The correct diagonal scheduling requires emitting slots from DIFFERENT groups at DIFFERENT rounds SIMULTANEOUSLY (true matrix diagonal traversal, not column-major).
attempts.key_learning: Correct diagonal = for d in range(32+16-1): for g in diagonal d: emit per_group_round_slots(g, d-g). This ensures group g round r and group g+1 round r-1 are adjacent in slot list → packer sees them together.

attempts.reasoning:
Round 9 root cause: group-major diagonal emission broke pair-safety. Groups g=0 and g=4 share pair p=g%4=0. When g=4 at round d-4 and g=0 at round d appear adjacent in the diagonal slot stream, their hash operations both write/read t1_pair[0], causing data corruption (9864 cycles). True diagonal requires per-group pair scratch = 32x4x8=1024 extra words; budget only has 105. Diagonal scheduling is NOT viable.

True remaining inefficiencies:
1. INIT SECTION (120 cycles): 32 groups x (alu+vload+alu+vload) through 2 shared addr_scalar registers. WAW on shared scalar forces ~64 cycles of serial execution (vs 16-cycle theoretical minimum for 32 vloads at 2/cycle). 
2. STORE SECTION (64 cycles): same pattern, 32 groups serialized through shared addr_scalar/addr_scalar2.
3. NOLOAD HEXTET XOR: 16 xors emitted in bulk (3 cycles) before any hash. Group A.xor completes cycle 1; A.hash[0] could start cycle 2 while later groups fill xor slots.

Fix 1+2: allocate per-group addr_idx_g[g] and addr_val_g[g] scalars (64 words, within 105-word budget). Emit all addr alu ops first (no WAW conflicts -> 3 bundles), then interleave vloads/vstores (2/cycle minimum). Saving: ~42c init + ~28c store = ~70c total.

Fix 3: reorder xor+hash emission in emit_hextet_noload to pipeline A.hash[0] at slot 7 (after first 6 xors). Saving: ~1-2c per hextet x 4 calls = ~16c.

Expected result: 2638 - 70 - 16 ≈ 2552c (well below 2600 success threshold).
attempts.key_learning: Round 9 diagonal failed due to pair scratch conflicts. True opportunity is in init/store section serialization via shared addr_scalars (64c wasted per section). Per-group addr_scalars (64 scratch words) unlocks this parallelism cleanly.

attempts.recommendation: IMPLEMENT_A_AND_B: setup const batching (-21c) + hextet tail xor injection (-25c)
attempts.key_learning: Fundamental hash dep chain barrier (depth 9, 14 extra cycles/hextet) prevents reaching 2164 threshold. Max achievable with current architecture: ~2508-2534. Setup has 21 avoidable wasted cycles from unbatched const loads. Hextet0 tail has 47 free valu slots that can absorb hextet1 xors in special rounds (saves 3c each) and normal rounds (saves 1c each). Load engine has 8 free slots in tail when intra_hextet_s used.
attempts.reasoning: Full bottleneck analysis: normal rounds 1822c (10*182c, overhead=54c/round from hash dep chain), special rounds 666c (6*111c, overhead=31c/round), setup 92c (21c wasted on unbatched consts). All RAW dep chains confirmed irreducible. Hextet overlap already maximized for loads. Unexplored: (1) setup const batching - trivial 21c savings; (2) xor injection into hextet0 tail free valu slots - 25c savings; (3) free load slots in intra_tail_next_s - 8c savings. Combined max ~46-72c improvement projects to 2508-2534. CANNOT reach 2164 threshold with any combination of these improvements alone.

attempts.vm_study: Planner read frozen_problem.py and perf_takehome.py in full
attempts.reasoning:
Three independent insights from source analysis:

1. DEAD SCRATCH (Direction A): lsb_tmp_pair[0..3] and cmp_tmp_pair[0..3] are allocated (64 words) but NEVER USED — Round 011 replaced them with addr_tmp_g[g] reuse. These 64 freed words enable doubling hash temp pairs from 4 to 8, eliminating pair contention between groups A-D and E-H within each hextet. Hash phase serial depth cuts from 40 to 20 cycles per hextet.

2. LOAD WASTE (Direction B): Round 010 trace showed 52.3% LOAD utilization — load engine is idle 47.7% of cycles despite being the throughput bottleneck. With 128 load_offset ops per hextet needing 64 cycles at 2/cycle, but taking ~122 cycles, there are ~58 wasted load-slot-cycles per hextet. Load ops from different groups are always independent, so a bulk-addr-then-bulk-load emission order should saturate the load engine.

3. TREE DEPTH PATTERN (Direction C): The tree height=7 means all depth-7 elements wrap to root after traversal. Round 8 is IDENTICAL to round 0 in tree access pattern, rounds 9,10 identical to 1,2. But code uses rnd%11 not rnd%8, sending rounds 8,9,10 down the slow full-load path. Fix this AND add round 3 special case: round 3 always accesses exactly 8 nodes (7-14 = VLEN), enabling a single vload instruction to fetch all needed values.

Most promising: Direction A (doubled pairs) — directly attacks the identified serialization bottleneck with a concrete mechanism. Direction B (bulk load) directly attacks the measured 52.3% LOAD waste.

attempts.summary: Disabled arith4 path for round_mod==2 (was 174 cycles, now 157 cycles normal path). Added prefetch support to emit_hextet_vselect2 so round_mod==1 hextets prefetch round_mod==2 loads. Result: 34 cycle improvement (2555->2521).

optimizations: mod1_inline_prefetch into mod0 noload hextet idle slots

factual_analysis.bottleneck: valu (70.6% utilization, 355 idle cycles vs 1044 load idle) — valu still dominant

attempts.unique_contribution: 8-pair 并行 hash+idx，parallel octet 重构，2555→2396 cycles

attempts.reasoning:
Round 12a trace: load=56.3% util (1044 idle cycles), valu=70.6% (355 idle), alu=0.5% (2380 idle).

PRIMARY DIRECTION: Exploit fixed-address prefetch windows in special rounds.
- Rounds mod0 (round 0 and 11): ~63 cycles each, load engine completely idle. These are broadcast-xor-hash-idx rounds with NO memory loads.
- Round mod1 (round 1, 12): needs nodes[1] and nodes[2] from tree. Addresses = forest_values_p+1 and +2. INDEPENDENT of any computed idx values.
- Round mod2 (round 2, 13): needs nodes[3..6]. Addresses = forest_values_p+3..+6. Also independent of computed idx.
- Therefore: during mod0 round 0's 63 idle load cycles, we can use alu (address arithmetic) + load (node fetches) to prefetch nodes 1,2 for round 1 AND nodes 3-6 for round 2 -- zero cycle cost since alu and load are both completely idle.
- Same applies during mod0 round 11: prefetch for rounds 12 and 13 (if rounds >= 14).
- This eliminates the entire setup overhead of mod1 and mod2 rounds (currently 5-6 cycles each for alu addr + load + broadcast setup).

SECONDARY DIRECTION: Store phase address redundancy.
- addr_idx_g[g] = inp_indices_p + g*VLEN is computed during init and never overwritten in the main loop.
- The store phase recomputes these 64 alu ops redundantly. Eliminating them saves ~5-6 cycles.

REJECTED DIRECTION: Group-major diagonal.
- ROUND_PLAN_9.json proposed group-major ordering claiming packer would interleave group 0 loads with groups 1-31 valu ops.
- This is WRONG. build() is a single-pass greedy packer. Group-major emission means groups 1-31 round 0-2 valu ops are emitted AND packed into bundles BEFORE group 0 round 3 loads appear. Packer cannot retroactively fill those bundles. Round 9 result of 9864 cycles confirms this catastrophic failure.
- The new ROUND_PLAN_9 repeats the same analysis error.

ALU UTILIZATION NOTE: ALU (12 slots/cycle) is almost entirely idle. The address arithmetic for special round setup (normally done with alu in 1 bundle) can be injected into the tail/idle phases of preceding rounds at zero cost. valu ops are already vectors so they cannot move to alu.
attempts.trace_basis: Round 12a: load 56.3% util, 1044 idle cycles; valu 70.6% util, 355 idle cycles; alu 0.5% util, 2380 idle cycles

special_rounds_analysis.fixed_addresses: mod1 needs nodes[1,2] at forest_values_p+1..+2; mod2 needs nodes[3..6] at forest_values_p+3..+6
special_rounds_analysis.estimated_savings: ~12-20 cycles from prefetch + ~5-6 cycles from store addr elim = ~17-26 cycles total

attempts.primary_bottleneck: load (1033 idle cycles, 56.6% utilization) and valu (348 idle cycles, 70.9% utilization)

attempts.reasoning: Round 13 trace: load 56.6% util (1033 idle), valu 70.9% (348 idle), alu 0.3% (2377 idle). Deep analysis reveals: load engine is at 99.9% saturation WHEN active (2700 slots / 1352 active cycles × 2 = 99.9%). The 1033 idle cycles are structurally forced -- they occur during hash+idx phases when no address for next-group load is yet known. Similarly, the 348 valu idle cycles occur during load-active phases where only 1 XOR/group occupies the valu. The current 16-group hextet pipeline is theoretically near-optimal for the present algorithm structure. Breaking through requires either: (A) extending the cross-round prefetch to cover more groups (A-H instead of A-D) in hextet tails, reducing startup overhead of next round -- load engine is idle for ~14 cycles in hextet1 tail, currently only 24/28 slots filled; (B) investigating whether tree nodes can be partially cached in scratch (185 words free), converting some load_offset ops to free valu operand reads; (C) exploring whether the hash or idx-update dependency chain can be shortened. Three parallel generators explore these mutually distinct directions. Diversification mode triggered by plateau (3 rounds with <3% improvement: 0.46%, 3.6%, 6.2%).
attempts.trace_basis: Round 13: load.idle=1033 (56.6% util, 2700 slots / 1352 active_cycles), valu.idle=348 (70.9%, 10150 slots / 2037 active_cycles), alu.idle=2377
attempts.load_analysis: load_slots_used=2700, load_max_possible=1352*2=2704. Load engine nearly perfectly saturated when active. Idle cycles are structurally unavoidable under current algorithm -- they are hash+idx cycles with no pending loads.
attempts.valu_analysis: valu_slots_used=10150, valu_active_cycles=2037, avg=4.98/cycle vs max 6. Gap=~2078 slot-opportunities. Occurs during early pipeline stages where load+valu overlap but only 1 xor per group per load phase.
attempts.estimated_potential: 014a: ~30-50 cycles if 4 more groups prefetched per round × 9 normal rounds; 014b: variable depending on how many loads can be eliminated; 014c: uncertain, hash chain depth is fundamental

attempts.acceptance_reason: 014c achieved 2357 cycles (1.17% improvement over 2385). All three candidates independently verified by Evaluator. Generator self-reports confirmed accurate. Winner accepted on positive improvement basis.
attempts.primary_bottleneck: valu engine is the tightest at 71.8% utilization with only 319 idle cycles. load engine has 1005 idle cycles suggesting further scheduling opportunities. store engine remains heavily underutilized (2325 idle cycles, 1.4% util) despite this round's improvement injecting stores into hextet tail.
